response.task: Create an ELAR/PARADISEC-compatible archive for a Papuan language with <200 speakers, integrating field recordings, interlinear glosses, paradigm data from 6 workers using 4 transcription conventions, and producing FLEx-compatible lexical database, typological grammatical sketch, curated time‑aligned texts, cross‑references, consistency checks, and rich metadata.

solution_steps.title: Collect raw audio & transcriptions
solution_steps.description: Gather WAV/MP3 files from all six fieldworkers. For each recording, obtain four transcription files: IPA narrow, IPA broad, practical orthography, and ad‑hoc romanization. Tag each file with speaker ID, date, location, and dialect note.

solution_steps.title: Standardise naming & directory structure
solution_steps.description: Create a hierarchical folder layout following ELAR/PARADISEC guidelines: /audio/, /transcriptions/IPA_narrow/, /transcriptions/IPA_broad/, /transcriptions/orthography/, /transcriptions/romanization/. Use machine‑readable filenames (e.g., speakerID_date_utterance.wav).

solution_steps.title: Convert to time‑aligned annotation format
solution_steps.description: Transform all transcriptions into CHAT or ELAN XML, adding precise timestamps. Align each utterance with the corresponding audio segment using a script that parses the original transcription files.

solution_steps.title: Build interlinear glossed text corpus
solution_steps.description: Create an IGT dataset in TEI or CLDF format. Map morphemes to glosses, lemmas, part of speech, and morphological analysis. Include fields for orthography, IPA (both narrow & broad), and pragmatic notes.

solution_steps.title: Extract paradigm data into FLEx
solution_steps.description: Compile verb conjugations, noun case paradigms, etc., into a spreadsheet. Import the spreadsheet into FLEx, generating lexical entries with full paradigm tables, phonological forms, glosses, and semantic fields.

solution_steps.title: Validate morpheme segmentation
solution_steps.description: Run an automated consistency check (Python script) that compares segment boundaries across the four transcription conventions. Generate a discrepancy report; resolve conflicts by consensus and update the master IGT corpus.

solution_steps.title: Create typological grammatical sketch
solution_steps.description: Using LaTeX or XML, draft sections for morphophonology, argument structure, clause combining, and information structure. Populate each section with data extracted from the corpus, citing lexical entries and text attestations.

solution_steps.title: Cross‑reference lexicon & texts
solution_steps.description: Link FLEx lemma IDs to IGT lines and ELAN time segments via unique identifiers. Ensure that clicking a word in FLEx opens the corresponding audio segment in ELAN, facilitating verification.

solution_steps.title: Compile metadata layer
solution_steps.description: Create a JSON/CSV file following PARADISEC’s metadata schema. Include speaker consent status, recording conditions (microphone type, environment), and dialectal variation notes for each audio file.

solution_steps.title: Package archive for submission
solution_steps.description: Bundle all materials into a single ELAR/PARADISEC submission package: /audio/, /transcriptions/, /annotations/, /lexicon/, /grammar/, /metadata/. Add README, license (e.g., CC BY-ND), and citation information. Verify that the archive meets technical and ethical requirements.

output.internal_reasoning: The solution follows ELAR/PARADISEC standards, integrates all required data types, and includes explicit steps for consistency checks and metadata. Confidence is high because the plan aligns with established archival practices.
